Initializes the autocompletion. The 'completer' has to be a function
with one argument (the current value of the control, i.e. the query)

modules.name: bsmutility.debugger
documentation: The code is based on PythonTookit (http://pythontoolkit.sourceforge.net/)

classes.name: bsmutility.debugger.PseudoEvent
documentation:
An object with the same interface as a threading.Event for the internal
engine. This prevents the readline/readlines and debugger from blocking
when waiting for user input.

This calls the doyield function and sleeps until the event is set.

methods.name: bsmutility.debugger.EngineDebugger.reset
documentation: Reset the debugger internal state
Interface methods

methods.name: bsmutility.debugger.EngineDebugger.stop_code
documentation:
Attempt to stop the running code by raising a keyboard interrupt in

methods.name: bsmutility.debugger.EngineDebugger.end
documentation: End the debugging and finish running the code

methods.name: bsmutility.debugger.EngineDebugger.pause
documentation: Pause currently running code - returns success

methods.name: bsmutility.debugger.EngineDebugger.resume
documentation: Unpause currently running code

methods.name: bsmutility.debugger.EngineDebugger.step
documentation: Step to the next line of code when the debugger is paused

methods.name: bsmutility.debugger.EngineDebugger.step_in
documentation:
Step into the new scope (function/callable).
This is only available if the next line is a 'call' event

methods.name: bsmutility.debugger.EngineDebugger.step_out
documentation:
Step out of a scope (function/callable).
This is only available if the debugger is currently in a scope other
than the main user namespace.

methods.name: bsmutility.debugger.EngineDebugger.set_breakpoint
documentation: breakpoints

methods.name: bsmutility.debugger.EngineDebugger.get_breakpoint
documentation:
Return the break point according to the (filename, lineno),
if not found, return None

methods.name: bsmutility.debugger.EngineDebugger.clear_breakpoint
documentation:
Clear the debugger breakpoint with the id given.
If id is None all breakpoints will be cleared.

methods.name: bsmutility.debugger.EngineDebugger.edit_breakpoint
documentation:
Edit a breakpoint.

Only modify the keywords given (from filename, lineno, condition,
trigger_count and ignore_count).

e.g. edit_breakpoint(id=1, filename='test.py', lineno=23) will modify
the breakpoint filename and lineno.

methods.name: bsmutility.debugger.EngineDebugger.get_scope
documentation:
Get the scope name,frame, globals and locals dictionaries for the scope at the
level given (None=current scope, 0=user namespace dictionary).

Note: To make any changes permanent you should call
_update_frame_locals(frame), with the frame.
debugger command/interogration interface

methods.name: bsmutility.debugger.EngineDebugger.set_scope
documentation:
Set the scope level to use for interogration when paused. This will be
reset at the next pause (i.e. after stepping).

methods.name: bsmutility.debugger.EngineDebugger.get_status
documentation: return the current status

methods.name: bsmutility.debugger.EngineDebugger.push_line
documentation:
A debugger commands to run - this is called from the engine when a
line is pushed and we are debugging and paused, so store internally and
wake the debugger.

methods.name: bsmutility.debugger.EngineDebugger.set_block_file
documentation:
Set a filepath to block from tracing.
use path* to include multiple files.
filepaths

methods.name: bsmutility.debugger.EngineDebugger.get_block_files
documentation: Get the filepaths that are currently blocked from tracing

methods.name: bsmutility.debugger.EngineDebugger.evaluate
documentation:
Evaluate expression in the active debugger scope and return result
(like builtin eval)

It is intened to be used to provide functionality to the GUI, so commands
should be fairly quick to process to avoid blocking.

The optional level argument controls the scope that will be used.
scope=None uses the current debugger scope and scope=0 is the top level
scope (the usernamespace).

Returns None on errors
engine like interfaces

methods.name: bsmutility.debugger.EngineDebugger.execute
documentation:
Execute expression in engine (like builtin exec)

It is intened to be used to provide functionality to the GUI interface,
so commands should be fairly quick to process to avoid blocking the
communications thread.

The optional level argument controls the scope that will be used.
scope=None uses the current debugger scope and scope=0 is the top level
scope (the usernamespace).

methods.name: bsmutility.debugger.EngineDebugger.__call__
documentation: This trace function is called only once when debugging starts.
trace methods
Trace every line - why?
1) It allows step out to work from a breakpoint in a nested scope
2) It makes the code easier to follow
3) It allows running code to be paused anywhere rather than just in traced scopes
The downside is speed, with every line now causing a call into python code

methods.name: bsmutility.debugger.EngineDebugger._trace_global
documentation: The main trace function called on call events

methods.name: bsmutility.debugger.EngineDebugger._trace_local
documentation:
The local trace function
- the main local trace function checks for breakpoints and pause requests
- used for all traced scopes unless stepping out, or ending debugging.

methods.name: bsmutility.debugger.EngineDebugger._trace_stepout
documentation:
A minimial local trace function used when stepping out of a scope.
- it will only pause if a breakpoint is encountered (it passes control back to trace_local)

methods.name: bsmutility.debugger.EngineDebugger._trace_pause
documentation:
A function called from within the local trace
function to handle a pauses at this event

methods.name: bsmutility.debugger.EngineDebugger._trace_off
documentation:
A dummy tracing function used when ending tracing as returning None
doesn't work (python bug v2.7 as of 01/2011)

methods.name: bsmutility.debugger.EngineDebugger._check_bp
documentation:
Decide whether to break at the bpdata given.
The frame is used to evaluate any conditons.
Internal methods

methods.name: bsmutility.debugger.EngineDebugger._hit_bp
documentation:
Decide whether to break at the bpdata given and update internal counters
and pause if necesary. The frame is used to evaluate any conditons.

_hit_bp (this method) will check, update counters and pause if necessary
_check_bp will only check - not pause or update counters.

documentation: Ensures changes to a frames locals are stored so they are not lost.

classes.name: bsmutility.debugger.DictList

methods.name: bsmutility.debugger.DictList.__init__
documentation:
Create a list like container object for dictionaries with the ability to
look up dicts by key value or by index.

dicts - is a sequence of dictionaries to populate the DictList with.
default - a defualt dictionary to use to create a new dictionary in the
list.

Example.

```pycon
>>> person1 = {'Name':'John', 'Age':53}
>>> person2 = {'Name':'Bob', 'Age':53}
>>> person3 = {'Name':'Sally', 'Age':31}
>>> default = {'Name':'', 'Age':0}
>>> dlist = DictList((person1,person2,person3),default)
```

```pycon
>>> #Filter by age
>>> dlist.filter(keys=('Age',),values=(53,))
[{'Age': 53, 'Name': 'John'}, {'Age': 53, 'Name': 'Bob'}]
```

```pycon
>>> #Filter by age and name
>>> dlist.filter(keys=('Age','Name'),values=(53,'John'))
[{'Age': 53, 'Name': 'John'}]
```

```pycon
>>> #Add a new dictionary
>>> dlist.new()
{'Age': 0, 'Name': ''}
```

methods.name: bsmutility.debugger.DictList.remove

methods.name: bsmutility.debugger.DictList._filter
documentation: internal method to find matching items
A general purpose list type object used for storing dictionaries these can
then be filtered by key.
Used to store breakpoints in both the engine debugger (engine process) and
engine manager(gui process) where breakpoints are stored as dictionaries

methods.name: bsmutility.debugger.EngineCompiler.compile
documentation:
Compile source.
Returns (more,code,err) where:

more        -   Bool indicating whether more is expected
code        -   the compiled code object if any
err         -   True if syntax error, false otherwise

methods.name: bsmutility.debugger.EngineCompiler.show_syntaxerror
documentation:
Display the syntax error that just occurred.
This doesn't display a stack trace because there isn't one.

If a filename is given, it is stuffed in the exception instead
of what was there before (because Python's parser always uses
"<string>" when reading from a string).

taken from: code.InteractiveInterpreter

methods.name: bsmutility.debugger.EngineCompiler.show_traceback
documentation:
Display the exception that just occurred.
We remove the first stack item because it is our own code and adjust the
line numbers for any engine inputs.
modified from: code.InteractiveInterpreter

methods.name: bsmutility.findlistctrl.ListCtrlAutoWidthMixin2._doResize
documentation:
Resize the last column as appropriate.

If the list's columns are too wide to fit within the window, we use
a horizontal scrollbar.  Otherwise, we expand the right-most column
to take up the remaining free space in the list.

We remember the current size of the last column, before resizing,
as the preferred minimum width if we haven't previously been given
or calculated a minimum width.  This ensure that repeated calls to
_doResize() don't cause the last column to size itself too large.

modules.name: bsmutility.pymgr_helpers
documentation: Manage windows (originated from matplotlib).

classes.name: bsmutility.pymgr_helpers.Gcm

methods.name: bsmutility.pymgr_helpers.Gcm.get_manager
documentation:
If manager *num* exists, make it active
and return the manager; otherwise return *None*.

methods.name: bsmutility.pymgr_helpers.Gcm.destroy
documentation:
Try to remove all traces of manager *num*.

In the interactive backends, this is bound to the
window "destroy" and "delete" events.

methods.name: bsmutility.pymgr_helpers.Gcm.destroy_all
documentation: remove all the managers

methods.name: bsmutility.pymgr_helpers.Gcm.has_num
documentation: Return *True* if manager *num* exists.

methods.name: bsmutility.pymgr_helpers.Gcm.get_all_managers
documentation: Return a list of managers.

methods.name: bsmutility.pymgr_helpers.Gcm.get_num_managers
documentation: Return the number of manager being managed.

methods.name: bsmutility.pymgr_helpers.Gcm.get_active
documentation: Return the manager of the active manager, or *None*.

methods.name: bsmutility.pymgr_helpers.Gcm.set_active
documentation: Make the mgr corresponding to *manager* the active one.

methods.name: bsmutility.pymgr_helpers.Gcm.get_nums
documentation: return all the occupied nums

methods.name: bsmutility.pymgr_helpers.Gcm.get_next_num
documentation: return the next available num

It consists of two class
attributes (a list and a dictionary), and a set of static
methods that operate on those attributes, accessing them
directly as class attributes.

Attributes:

    *mgrs*:
      dictionary of the form {*num*: *manager*, ...}

    *_activeQue*:
      list of *managers*, with active one at the end

classes.name: bsmutility.dirtreectrl.Directory
documentation: Simple class for using as the data object in the DirTreeCtrl

classes.name: bsmutility.dirtreectrl.DirMixin

methods.name: bsmutility.dirtreectrl.DirMixin.addIcon
bsmutility.dirtreectrl.DirMixin.LoadPath
documentation:
Private function that gets called to load the file list
for the given directory and append the items to the tree.
Throws an exception if the directory is invalid.

@note: does not add items if the node already has children

methods.name: bsmutility.dirtreectrl.DirMixin.getFileExtension
documentation: Helper function for getting a file's extension

methods.name: bsmutility.dirtreectrl.DirMixin.processFileExtension
documentation:
Helper function. Called for files and collects all the necessary
icons into in image list which is re-passed into the tree every time
(imagelists are a lame way to handle images)

methods.name: bsmutility.dirtreectrl.DirMixin.SendDirEvent
documentation: send the property event to the parent
helper class to handle files/folders in a folder

methods.name: bsmutility.dirtreectrl.DirTreeMixin.SetDeleteOnCollapse
documentation:
Sets the tree option to delete leaf items when the node is
collapsed. Will slow down the tree slightly but will probably save memory.

methods.name: bsmutility.dirtreectrl.DirTreeMixin.TreeItemExpanding
documentation:
Called when a node is about to expand. Loads the node's
files from the file system.

methods.name: bsmutility.dirtreectrl.DirTreeMixin.TreeItemCollapsing

classes.name: bsmutility.dirtreectrl.DirTreeCtrl
documentation:
A wx.TreeCtrl that is used for displaying directory structures.
Virtually handles paths to help with memory management.

classes.name: bsmutility.dirtreectrl.DirListCtrl
documentation:
A wx.ListCtrl that is used for displaying directory structures.
Virtually handles paths to help with memory management.

classes.name: bsmutility.dirtreectrl.DirTreeList
documentation:
A HTL.HyperTreeList that is used for displaying directory structures.
Virtually handles paths to help with memory management.
